response.justification: Experienced software engineer with 17+ years of delivery and strong architectural thinking, but misaligned with role requirements. Last significant C++ work was ~10 years ago (National Instruments, 2015); recent trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js. No demonstrable experience in industrial automation, embedded systems design, IoT, or IEC 62443 security standards—all critical for this manufacturing-focused role. While agile and mentoring capabilities are strong, the technical gaps would require extensive onboarding.
strengths: 17+ years of software experience exceeding the 8-12 year requirement, with proven ability to architect complex, high-performance distributed systems
Strong software craftsmanship foundation with TDD, agile methodologies, mentoring, and event-driven architecture experience (GISCollective)
Demonstrated ability to work on performance-critical systems (Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second) and learn new technology stacks rapidly
concerns: C++ expertise is outdated with last significant work ~10 years ago (6 months at National Instruments in 2015); primary focus now TypeScript/Node.js/web development
Zero evidence of industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or manufacturing domain knowledge—critical for this automation machinery company role
Missing key technical requirements: industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), MQTT protocol, multi-threading/concurrent design patterns, and Secure Development Lifecycle practices

key_factors.factor: C++ currency and embedded systems background
Industrial automation and security standards knowledge
